description:
Organize a 16-panel solar array into two electrical strings (West and East) by assigning them to designated layers and color-coding them.

Starting State:
- SketchUp Make 2017 is open with a model containing a south-facing array of 16 solar panels (2 rows of 8 columns).
- The panels are arranged horizontally: The left side is West, the right side is East.

Actions to take:
1. Open the Layers manager and create two new layers named: `String_West` and `String_East`.
2. Select the 8 panels on the left (West) side of the array (you may need to enter the array group first).
3. Assign these 8 West panels to the layer `String_West`.
4. Paint these 8 West panels Red.
5. Select the 8 panels on the right (East) side of the array.
6. Assign these 8 East panels to the layer `String_East`.
7. Paint these 8 East panels Blue.
8. Save the completed model to `C:\Users\Docker\Documents\stringing_complete.skp`.
